The query asks for the production company founded by George Balanchine to create a live stage version of the 1942 film Casablanca.

- Use the tools to gather evidence and cross-reference the findings.

While Balanchine was a prolific choreographer and co-founded several significant ballet companies, including the New York City Ballet, there is no record of him being involved in a stage adaptation of *Casablanca*[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City_Ballet), [2](https://www.abt.org/wp-content/uploads/ABT-Press/PressKits/Balanchine_George.pdf), [3](https://www.nycballet.com/discover/our-history/george-balanchine), [4](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/George_Balanchine), [5](https://www.kennedy-center.org/artists/b/ba-bn/george-balanchine/), [6](https://www.balanchine.com/george-balanchine), [7](https://www.balletmet.org/who-is-george-balanchine/), [8](https://www.theguardian.com/stage/2009/jul/30/dance-george-balanchine). He did choreograph for some Hollywood films and Broadway productions during his career[4](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/George_Balanchine), [6](https://www.balanchine.com/george-balanchine), [7](https://www.balletmet.org/who-is-george-balanchine/).

A dance musical titled *CASABLANCA* premiered in 2005, directed and choreographed by John Clifford, who is described as a protégé of George Balanchine. This production was financed by Warner Bros. Theatre Ventures, Inc., and first performed in Beijing, China, well after Balanchine's time.[9](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_Ea5YtnP64w)"]}}

Conclusion: STOP

The memory indicates that George Balanchine did not found a production company to create a live stage version of the 1942 film *Casablanca*. Instead, a dance musical titled *CASABLANCA* premiered in 2005, directed and choreographed by John Clifford, who is described as a protégé of George Balanchine. This production was financed by Warner Bros. Theatre Ventures, Inc. The information provided is clear and consistent, with no need for additional verification or clarification. Therefore, the memory is complete and accurate enough to answer the query.

result:
George Balanchine did not found a production company to create a live stage version of the 1942 film *Casablanca*.

While Balanchine was a prolific choreographer and co-founded several significant ballet companies, including the New York City Ballet, there is no record of him being involved in a stage adaptation of *Casablanca*[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City_Ballet), [2](https://www.abt.org/wp-content/uploads/ABT-Press/PressKits/Balanchine_George.pdf), [3](https://www.nycballet.com/discover/our-history/george-balanchine), [4](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/George_Balanchine), [5](https://www.kennedy-center.org/artists/b/ba-bn/george-balanchine/), [6](https://www.balanchine.com/george-balanchine), [7](https://www.balletmet.org/who-is-george-balanchine/), [8](https://www.theguardian.com/stage/2009/jul/30/dance-george-balanchine). He did choreograph for some Hollywood films and Broadway productions during his career[4](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/George_Balanchine), [6](https://www.balanchine.com/george-balanchine), [7](https://www.balletmet.org/who-is-george-balanchine/).

A dance musical titled *CASABLANCA* premiered in 2005, directed and choreographed by John Clifford, who is described as a protégé of George Balanchine. This production was financed by Warner Bros. Theatre Ventures, Inc., and first performed in Beijing, China, well after Balanchine's time.[9](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_Ea5YtnP64w)

direct_output:
<answer>
George Balanchine did not found a production company to create a live stage version of the 1942 film Casablanca. However, a dance musical titled CASABLANCA, inspired by the film, premiered in 2005, directed and choreographed by John Clifford, a protégé of George Balanchine. This production was financed by Warner Bros. Theatre Ventures, Inc.
</answer>
